merger_name: B.M.D. Holdings – Bajara Plant Hire and Stirling Contracting

anzsic_codes.name: Other Heavy and Civil Engineering Construction
Hire of Construction Machinery with Operator
Heavy Machinery and Scaffolding Rental and Hiring
Engineering Design and Engineering Consulting Services

merger_description:
B.M.D. Holdings Pty. Limited the ultimate holding company of ‘BMD Group’ (the **Acquirer**) proposes to acquire a minority equity position in each of Bajara Plant Hire Pty Ltd (**Bajara**) and Stirling Contracting Pty Ltd (a newly incorporated entity that will acquire the assets of the earthworks business currently owned by Stirling Earthworks Pty Ltd, Stirling) (together, the **Target**) by way of Share Purchase Agreement (**Proposed Acquisition**).

BMD Group operates across civil construction, engineering design, residential and industrial land development, project management consultancy, landscape construction and maintenance, and building construction. Headquartered in Brisbane, it operates nationwide across Australia.

Relevantly, ‘BMD Constructions’ is BMD Group’s construction arm. It delivers complex infrastructure projects across sectors including defence, water and wastewater, transport, ports, aviation and renewable energy; through the provision of civil and building works for land development and associated infrastructure. BMD self-supplies both plant and equipment, and earthworks services but does not offer these services to third parties.

The Target operates two businesses in the construction industry:

1. Bajara, which supplies civil construction and mining spec plant and equipment for both wet and dry hire; and
2. Stirling, which supplies earthworks services.

Also headquartered in Brisbane, the Target delivers small-to-medium-sized infrastructure and resources projects predominantly in Queensland, predominantly in Queensland, and including to BMD.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
B.M.D. Holdings Pty. Limited the ultimate holding company of BMD
Group (the Acquirer) applied for a notification waiver in respect to its
proposed acquisition of a minority equity position in each of Bajara
Plant Hire Pty Ltd (Bajara) and Stirling Contracting Pty Ltd (a newly
incorporated entity that will acquire the assets of the earthworks
business currently owned by Stirling Earthworks Pty Ltd, Stirling)
(together, the Target) by way of Share Purchase Agreement
(Proposed Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The Acquirer, BMD Group operates across civil construction,
engineering design, residential and industrial land development,
project management consultancy, landscape construction and
maintenance, and building construction. Headquartered in Brisbane,
it operates nationwide across Australia.
Relevantly, ‘BMD Constructions’ is BMD Group’s construction arm. It
delivers complex infrastructure projects across sectors including
defence, water and wastewater, transport, ports, aviation and
renewable energy; through the provision of civil and building works
for land development and associated infrastructure. BMD self-
supplies both plant and equipment, and earthworks services but
does not offer these services to third parties.
The Target operates two businesses in the construction industry:
1. Bajara, which supplies civil construction and mining-specific
plant and equipment for both wet and dry hire; and
2. Stirling, which supplies earthworks services.
Also headquartered in Brisbane, the Target delivers small-to-
medium-sized infrastructure and resources projects, predominantly
in Queensland, and including to BMD.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is limited horizontal overlap between the parties in the
supply of plant and equipment hire for civil construction and
mining, or earthworks services in Queensland
b. there are alternative suppliers of plant and equipment hire
for civil construction and mining, and earthworks services in
Queensland
c. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical
effects resulting from exclusionary conduct post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
